title: Anatomy of a megathrust: The 2010 M8.8 Maule, Chile earthquake rupture zone imaged using seismic tomography
abstract: Aftershocks from onshore seismic stations and ocean bottom seismometers are manually analysed to derive a 3-D velocity model of the 2010 Maule earthquake rupture zone using local earthquake tomography. An east-dipping high P-wave velocity anomaly (>6.9 km/s) is interpreted as the subducting oceanic crust. A subducted topographic high at 25 km depth coincides with a strong forearc Bouguer gravity signal, interpreted as a former seamount on the Nazca slab.

location.description: 2010 Maule earthquake rupture zone, central Chile ~34°–38°S, onshore-offshore seismic network

data.name: Maule Aftershock OBS Passive Seismic Data — Central Chile ~34°–38°S
data.description: OBS passive seismic recordings of Maule earthquake aftershocks used for 3D local earthquake tomography; combined with onshore network to derive 3D Vp model of the rupture zone

data.name: Bouguer Gravity — Maule Rupture Zone
data.description: Forearc Bouguer gravity data used to corroborate velocity anomaly interpreted as subducted seamount at 25 km depth

_data_note: Passive OBS deployment post-Maule earthquake (2010). Seismic waveforms may be at IRIS/EarthScope. No active-source MCS data acquired.

processing.summary: Passive OBS seismic data from ocean bottom seismometers deployed after the 2010 Maule earthquake were combined with onshore network arrivals to derive a 3D P-wave velocity model via local earthquake tomography. The 3D model images the subducting Nazca plate as an east-dipping high-velocity anomaly, and identifies a former seamount on the slab at 25 km depth coinciding with a Bouguer gravity high in the forearc.
workflow: 1. Manual P-wave arrival time picking on OBS and onshore station data
3. 3D simultaneous inversion for hypocenters and velocity structure
processing.notes: Passive seismic study; OBS component provides offshore constraints on shallow subduction geometry

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Open access CC BY-NC-ND; open_access_url set. Authors 5 confirmed. 27 OBS total: 17 Taiwanese (National Taiwan Ocean University) + 10 UK (OBIC) + IMAD ~160 onshore stations. Vessels provided by Armada de Chile (not named). 669 earthquakes used; SDX for picking, HYPO71PC for location, VELEST for 1D inversion, SIMULPS for 3D tomography (staggered scheme). channel_count updated to 27. Vessel name clarified.
